sections.title: Gen AI and Lrg Language Models
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON SECTION (campus presence). Special Topics in CS: Generative AI and Large Language Model (LLMs). This course provides an exhaustive exploration of Generative Artificial Intelligence, with a focus on foundational mathematical principles, recent research breakthroughs, novel model architectures, and applications cutting across a wide range of domains. A variety of modern deep learning architectures, such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), Transformers, and Diffusion Models, will be covered in the course. The emphasis will be on practical aspects pertaining to the training and deployment of large models: data preparation, setting up training pipelines, evaluation of model performance, and strategies for improving training efficiency. Prerequisite: COMP 3105 or COMP 3106. Must have 4th year standing in BCS.

sections.title: Detail Augmentation & Synth
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON SECTION (campus presence). Special Topics in CS: Detail Augmentation and Synthesis. (Previously titled Texture and Pattern Synthesis. Students who took this course under the previous title may not take it again for credit.) This is a specialized course on image detail, studying mechanisms for enriching synthetic and photographic images with fine-scale structure. Major themes include texture, image processing, and generative AI. Throughout the term, we will discuss various methods and algorithms, and implement some; the main approach to the material is reading the primary literature and discussing specific paper selected by the instructor. Prerequisites: One of COMP 3009, COMP 3501, COMP 4509, COMP4900 (Special Topics-Computer Graphics) or COMP 4102. (COMP 4102 may be taken concurrently).

sections.title: Real-Time Operating Systems
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON SECTION (campus presence). Special Topics in CS: Real-Time Operating Systems. This course covers the basics of Real-Time Operating Systems (RTOS). The course focuses on the primary principles of RTOS. Topics include RTOS architecture, inter process communication, determinism, real-time scheduling, interrupt latency, fast context switching, time and space partitioning in hard real-time environments. Prerequisite: COMP 3000. SYSC 4001 may only be used as a prerequisite if taken prior to Fall 2025.

sections.title: Graph Analytics
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON SECTION (campus presence). Special Topics in CS: Graph Analytics. This course will present algorithmic and statistical techniques to analyze the strength and direction of connections between objects in a graph/network. Examples include graph clustering, community finding, infection tracking, fraud detection, and recommendation systems. Prerequisites: COMP 2402 and COMP 2804. COMP 3804 is a recommended prerequisite.

sections.title: Quantum Comm. & Networking
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON SECTION (campus presence). Selected Topics in CS: Quantum Communications and Networking. The use of individual photons and teleportation to represent and transmit information. Theoretical (mathematical) principles. Practical aspects (implementation and software simulation) of quantum communications and networking. Prerequisite: 4th year standing. Priority to BCS students.

sections.title: Reinforcement Learning
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON SECTION (campus presence). Special Topics in ML- Reinforcement Learning. Reinforcement learning (RL) focuses on learning by interact- ion with a complicated environment through trial and error. In this research-oriented course, students will first learn the fundamental concepts of RL, including Markov decision processes, value prediction, and optimal control. Then we will investigate several advanced topics in the recent RL literature, such as offline/batch RL, distributional RL and Priority will be given to OCICS joint institute students; and students in thesis-based; research programs. Precludes additional credit for COMP 5900 & COMP 5801 Introduction to Reinforcement Learning.

sections.title: Graphic Analytics
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON SECTION. Special Topics in CS: Graph Analytics. This course will present algorithmic and statistical techniques to analyze the strength and direction of Examples include graph clustering, community finding, connections between objects in a graph/network. infection tracking, fraud detection, and recommendation systems. Priority will be given to OCICS joint institute students and students in thesis-based research programs.

sections.title: Detail Augmentation & Synth
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON SECTION (campus presence). Special Topics in CS: Detail Augmentation and Synthesis. This is a specialized course on image detail, studying mechanisms for enriching synthetic and photographic images with fine-scale structure. Major themes include texture, image processing, and generative AI. Throughout the term, we will discuss various methods and algorithms, and implement some; the main approach to the material is reading the primary literature and discussing specific paper selected by the instructor. Precludes additional credit for COMP5900- Special Topics Title- Texture and Pattern Synthesis. Priority will be given to OCICS joint institute students and students in thesis-based research programs.

sections.title: Project-based Learning
sections.info: Section Type - IN-PERSON. Provides (1) an environment where TIM students in their 2nd or 3rd term can do research to add value to identifiable identifiable real-world clients and (2) an environment to develop TIM project proposals. The client may be a large firm, a small- or medium-sized company, an entrepreneur, or a Carleton department, faculty, research organization, or support group. Project proposals will follow the established TIM Gate Process for student research and will be presented at a TIM Gate Review.
